parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Optional. The maximum number of requests to return.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Optional. A pagination token from a previous call.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Optional. An expression for filtering the results.
Filterable fields:

+ `state`
+ `priority`
+ `assignee`
+ `displayName`
+ `creator`
+ `createTime` (supports comparison operators)
+ `dueTime` (supports comparison operators)

Examples:

+ `state = OPEN` — only open requests.
+ `assignee = "users/123"` — requests assigned to a user.
+ `priority = URGENT` — urgent requests only.
+ `state = IN_PROGRESS AND assignee = ""` — unassigned in-progress.

For more information, see [AIP-160](https://aip.dev/160).

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description:
Optional. A comma-separated list of fields to order by.
Supported fields:

+ `displayName`
+ `createTime`
+ `updateTime`
+ `dueTime`
+ `priority`
+ `name`

Example: `priority desc, dueTime`

parameters.name: showDeleted
parameters.description: Optional. If true, include soft-deleted requests in the response.

post.summary:
Creates a new request in the specified project. Line items can be
included in the request body — each line item creates a
PLACEHOLDER asset in the project.
post.description:
The caller must have `assets.requests.create` permission on the
parent project.

parameters.name: request
parameters.description:
Required. The request to create. May include line items in the
line_items field — each will create a PLACEHOLDER asset.

parameters.name: validateOnly
parameters.description: Optional. If set to true, validate only without persisting.

get.summary: Retrieves a request by resource name.

delete.summary:
Soft-deletes a request. Also soft-deletes any unfulfilled
PLACEHOLDER assets linked to its line items.

parameters.name: etag
parameters.description: Optional. The etag for optimistic concurrency.

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: Optional. If true, delete the request even if it has line items.

parameters.name: validateOnly
parameters.description: Optional. If set to true, validate only without persisting.

patch.summary:
Updates a request's mutable fields (display_name, description,
priority, due_time).

parameters.name: request
parameters.description: Required. The request with updated fields.

displayName.description: Required. A human-readable title for the request.

description.description: Optional. A detailed description of what is needed.

state.description: Output only. The current lifecycle state.

priority.description: Optional. The priority of the request.

deliveredTime.description: Output only. When the artist marked the request as delivered.

approvedTime.description: Output only. When the producer approved the request.

lineItemCount.description: Output only. Total number of line items.

fulfilledCount.description: Output only. Number of line items with fulfilled (ACTIVE) assets.

lineItems.description:
Output only. Line items belonging to this request. Populated on
GetRequest. Not populated on ListRequests for efficiency.

annotations.description: Optional. Annotations associated with this request.

etag.description: Output only. A checksum computed by the server.

creator.description: Output only. The user who created the request.

updater.description: Output only. The user who last updated the request.

createTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the request was created.

updateTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the request was last modified.

deleteTime.description: Output only. The time at which the request was soft-deleted.

purgeTime.description: Output only. The time at which the request will be permanently purged.

schema.title: Required. The request with updated fields.

parameters.name: validateOnly
parameters.description: Optional. If set to true, validate only without persisting.

get.summary: Lists line items in a request.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Optional. The maximum number of line items to return.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Optional. A pagination token from a previous call.

post.summary:
Creates a new line item on a request. A PLACEHOLDER asset is
created in the project and linked to the line item.

parameters.name: lineItem
parameters.description: Required. The line item to create.

parameters.name: validateOnly
parameters.description: Optional. If set to true, validate only without persisting.

get.summary: Retrieves a line item by resource name.

delete.summary:
Deletes a line item. Only allowed when the request is in DRAFT
or OPEN state. Also soft-deletes the linked PLACEHOLDER asset.

patch.summary: Updates a line item's mutable fields (display_name, description).

parameters.name: lineItem
parameters.description: Required. The line item with updated fields.

displayName.description: Required. A human-readable name for the deliverable.

description.description: Optional. A description of what is expected for this deliverable.

state.description: Output only. The current state of the line item.

asset.description:
Output only. The linked PLACEHOLDER asset. Created when the line
item is created. Transitions to ACTIVE when the artist fulfills
the line item.

mediaType.description: Optional. The expected media type for this deliverable.

annotations.description: Optional. Annotations associated with this line item.

creator.description: Output only. The user who created this line item.

createTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when this line item was created.

updateTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when this line item was last modified.

schema.title: Required. The line item with updated fields.

parameters.name: validateOnly
parameters.description: Optional. If set to true, validate only without persisting.

post.summary:
Fulfills a line item by uploading a deliverable. The linked
PLACEHOLDER asset transitions to PROCESSING and runs the
ingestion pipeline. Returns an LRO tracking the ingestion.

Approves the delivered request. Transitions the request from
DELIVERED to APPROVED.

Assigns an artist to the request. Transitions the request to
IN_PROGRESS if currently OPEN. Can also reassign during
IN_PROGRESS.

Cancels the request at any point. Transitions the request to
CANCELLED. Unfulfilled PLACEHOLDER assets remain but are not
deleted.

Self-assigns the calling user to an open request. Transitions the
request from OPEN to IN_PROGRESS with the caller as assignee.

Marks the request as delivered by the artist. Transitions the
request from IN_PROGRESS to DELIVERED.

Rejects the delivered request. Transitions the request from
DELIVERED to REJECTED.

Requests revisions from the artist. Transitions the request from
DELIVERED to REVISION_REQUESTED. The artist picks it up and it
moves back to IN_PROGRESS.

Submits a draft request, making it visible to artists.
Transitions the request from DRAFT to OPEN.

AssetMediaType.description:
The broad media category of the asset.

 - MEDIA_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified media type.
 - IMAGE: Still image (JPEG, PNG, TIFF, WebP, etc.).
 - VIDEO: Video file (MP4, MOV, MXF, ProRes, etc.).
 - AUDIO: Audio file (WAV, MP3, AAC, FLAC, etc.).
 - GRAPHIC: Motion graphics or vector (SVG, Lottie, Rive, etc.).
 - DOCUMENT: Document (PDF, PSD, AI, etc.).

RequestPriority.description:
Request priority levels.

 - PRIORITY_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified priority. Treated as NORMAL.
 - LOW: Low priority.
 - NORMAL: Normal priority (default).
 - HIGH: High priority.
 - URGENT: Urgent priority.

RequestsApproveRequestBody.description: The request sent to the ApproveRequest method.

assignee.title: Required. The user to assign. Format: `users/{user}`

RequestsAssignRequestBody.description: The request sent to the AssignRequest method.

RequestsCancelRequestBody.description: The request sent to the CancelRequest method.

RequestsClaimRequestBody.description: The request sent to the ClaimRequest method.

RequestsDeliverRequestBody.description: The request sent to the DeliverRequest method.

endpoint.description: Required. The storage endpoint to store the deliverable on.

filename.description: Optional. The original filename from the client.

sizeBytes.description: Optional. The file size in bytes. Required for multipart uploads.

RequestsFulfillLineItemBody.description: The request sent to the FulfillLineItem method.

rejectionReason.description: Optional. A note explaining why the request was rejected.

RequestsRejectRequestBody.description: The request sent to the RejectRequest method.

revisionNote.description: Optional. A note explaining what revisions are needed.

RequestsRequestRevisionBody.description: The request sent to the RequestRevision method.

RequestsSubmitRequestBody.description: The request sent to the SubmitRequest method.

name.description:
The server-assigned name, which is only unique within the same service that
originally returns it. If you use the default HTTP mapping, the
`name` should be a resource name ending with `operations/{unique_id}`.

metadata.description:
Service-specific metadata associated with the operation.  It typically
contains progress information and common metadata such as create time.
Some services might not provide such metadata.  Any method that returns a
long-running operation should document the metadata type, if any.

done.description:
If the value is `false`, it means the operation is still in progress.
If `true`, the operation is completed, and either `error` or `response` is
available.

error.description: The error result of the operation in case of failure or cancellation.

response.description:
The normal, successful response of the operation.  If the original
method returns no data on success, such as `Delete`, the response is
`google.protobuf.Empty`.  If the original method is standard
`Get`/`Create`/`Update`, the response should be the resource.  For other
methods, the response should have the type `XxxResponse`, where `Xxx`
is the original method name.  For example, if the original method name
is `TakeSnapshot()`, the inferred response type is
`TakeSnapshotResponse`.

longrunningOperation.description:
This resource represents a long-running operation that is the result of a
network API call.

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com. As of May 2023, there are no widely used type server
implementations and no plans to implement one.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

code.description:
The status code, which should be an enum value of
[google.rpc.Code][google.rpc.Code].

message.description:
A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any
user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the
[google.rpc.Status.details][google.rpc.Status.details] field, or localized
by the client.

details.description:
A list of messages that carry the error details.  There is a common set of
message types for APIs to use.

rpcStatus.description:
The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for
different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is
used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains
three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details.

You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the
[API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

name.title:
Output only. The resource name of the line item. Format:
`organizations/{organization}/projects/{project}/requests/{request}/lineItems/{line_item}`

displayName.description: Required. A human-readable name for the deliverable.

description.description: Optional. A description of what is expected for this deliverable.

state.description: Output only. The current state of the line item.

asset.description:
Output only. The linked PLACEHOLDER asset. Created when the line
item is created. Transitions to ACTIVE when the artist fulfills
the line item.

mediaType.description: Optional. The expected media type for this deliverable.

annotations.description: Optional. Annotations associated with this line item.

creator.description: Output only. The user who created this line item.

createTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when this line item was created.

updateTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when this line item was last modified.

v1LineItem.description:
A line item within a request. Each line item represents a single
deliverable and is linked to a PLACEHOLDER asset that is fulfilled
when the artist uploads the file.

v1LineItemState.description:
Line item states.

 - STATE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified state.
 - PENDING: Awaiting artist delivery.
 - IN_PROGRESS: Artist is working on this item.
 - DELIVERED: Artist uploaded the deliverable.
 - APPROVED: Producer approved this item.
 - REVISION_REQUESTED: Producer requested changes on this item.

lineItems.description: The line items belonging to the specified request.

nextPageToken.description: A pagination token for the next page.

v1ListLineItemsResponse.description: The response from the ListLineItems method.

requests.description: The requests belonging to the specified project.

nextPageToken.description: A pagination token for the next page.

v1ListRequestsResponse.description: The response from the ListRequests method.

name.title:
Output only. The resource name of the request. Format:
`organizations/{organization}/projects/{project}/requests/{request}`

displayName.description: Required. A human-readable title for the request.

description.description: Optional. A detailed description of what is needed.

state.description: Output only. The current lifecycle state.

priority.description: Optional. The priority of the request.

deliveredTime.description: Output only. When the artist marked the request as delivered.

approvedTime.description: Output only. When the producer approved the request.

lineItemCount.description: Output only. Total number of line items.

fulfilledCount.description: Output only. Number of line items with fulfilled (ACTIVE) assets.

lineItems.description:
Output only. Line items belonging to this request. Populated on
GetRequest. Not populated on ListRequests for efficiency.

annotations.description: Optional. Annotations associated with this request.

etag.description: Output only. A checksum computed by the server.

creator.description: Output only. The user who created the request.

updater.description: Output only. The user who last updated the request.

createTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the request was created.

updateTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the request was last modified.

deleteTime.description: Output only. The time at which the request was soft-deleted.

purgeTime.description: Output only. The time at which the request will be permanently purged.

v1Request.description:
A request represents an order for one or more assets. Producers
create requests, assign artists, and approve deliverables. Each
request contains line items that map to individual PLACEHOLDER
assets.

v1RequestState.description:
Request lifecycle states.

 - STATE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified state.
 - DRAFT: Producer is composing the request. Not visible to artists.
 - OPEN: Submitted and awaiting artist assignment or self-claim.
 - IN_PROGRESS: An artist is actively working on the request.
 - DELIVERED: The artist has delivered all line items for review.
 - APPROVED: The producer has approved the deliverables.
 - REVISION_REQUESTED: The producer has requested changes. The artist picks it up
and the request moves back to IN_PROGRESS.
 - REJECTED: The producer has rejected the deliverables.
 - CANCELLED: The request was cancelled.
